behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, because this is a static rule check over template text. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration was considered, but behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," so no module handoff, real OS/network/subprocess, or model output is required; System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified. FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure lint function with in-memory template strings: one with copied phase instructions from SKILL.md must fail, and one clean template must pass.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A lower-than-Unit level does not exist; higher levels add infra not needed for this failure mode.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since failure appears when template + interpolation + fixtures are wired. PASS2-AUDIT: System rejected because no real HTTP/subprocess/OS primitive is needed; Agentic/Workflow rejected because this checks prompt text, not model judgment. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly path with representative fixtures and assert the final prompt does not contain banned phase-instruction fragments.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit would miss cross-module leakage introduced during interpolation/fixture composition.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because it targets file-loader + invocation wiring + context injection boundaries. PASS2-AUDIT: Agentic considered, but rejected by behavior text "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" (infrastructure source-of-truth check, not model-quality check); System rejected since local file wiring is sufficient. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the harness with real plugin/skill files and assert the resolved context source is file-derived (not embedded prompt constants), using controlled fixtures that expose hard-coded fallback misuse.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit would not catch failures where loader and invocation layers disagree.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this is a harness parameterization/file-variant handoff behavior. PASS2-AUDIT: Agentic rejected due behavior text "without changing test prompt text" (harness capability, not model decision correctness); System rejected because local fixture/file substitution is enough. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two runs through the same harness code path and identical user prompt text, swapping only SKILL.md variant (normal vs degraded/missing), and verify the run configuration changes only via skill source selection.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit would miss integration bugs where prompt text mutates or variant selection is ignored.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because correctness depends on how the real model behavior changes when skill content regresses. PASS2-AUDIT: Workflow considered but rejected since behavior describes one prompt-response judgment, not multiple sequential model decisions; Integration rejected because stubs can hide true model-behavior regressions. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style prompt against intact vs degraded SKILL.md with live model execution; assert pass on intact and fail on degraded under identical prompt text and assertion rules.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower levels cannot verify model-behavior sensitivity to skill-content changes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since this is ordering across capture, parser, and artifact-writing modules. PASS2-AUDIT: System rejected because local file writes are enough (no real subprocess/network needed); Agentic rejected because this verifies pipeline order, not model semantic quality. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Inject malformed/non-JSON model output through the harness, assert raw artifact is written first, and confirm parser/extractor failure occurs after capture.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit can miss ordering bugs introduced by wiring between capture and parse stages.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, to prove the actual test assertion stack enforces structure. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit considered, but rejected because behavior text targets suite-level "Assertions enforce..." and wiring can bypass strict checks; Agentic/System/Workflow rejected because no real model or external infra is required to validate assertion semantics. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Feed crafted outputs where required keywords exist but structure is wrong (e.g., wrong path/role field), and verify the real test assertion path fails them.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This catches real failures where assertion helpers exist but are not what the harness enforces.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, explicitly required by behavior text "Under live model execution" and dependence on real model outputs. PASS2-AUDIT: Workflow rejected because this does not require multi-step agentic chaining; Integration rejected because deterministic mocks can miss real output-shape regressions seen only with live generation. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests with structural assertions and compare against a keyword-only oracle on the same outputs; verify cases that keyword-only passes but structural checks correctly fail.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Only live model execution exposes this regression class reliably.
